asset.title: Noderoom Live Collab V1
asset.purpose: Narrated explainer episode. Judge story clarity, visual design, proof quality, and whether it fits the README claim.

judge.summary: The video demonstrates multiplayer collaboration between humans and AI agents on a shared spreadsheet, highlighting conflict resolution and review workflows, though some technical slides pass too quickly.

featureClarity.evidence: The UI shows spreadsheet cells being edited by both a human and an agent, though the transition between modes is rapid.

workflowCompleteness.evidence: Shows the setup, conflict occurrence, code/architecture solution, and resolved state, but at a very fast pace.

visualDesign.evidence: Dark mode UI is clean and modern, but the text overlay and code slides have contrasting densities.

consistency.evidence: Aligns with the NodeRoom concept of shared files, agents, and versioned cell edits.

evidenceQuality.evidence: Shows actual UI interactions and code, though some parts feel highly produced or simulated.

legibility.evidence: Most UI elements are readable, but the code snippet at 00:18 is quite small.

professionalRelevance.evidence: Highly relevant for developers building multi-agent collaborative systems and transactional workspaces.

productionHonesty.evidence: Clearly states the transition from a simple demo to a complex collaboration substrate, showing the underlying code logic.

observedEvidence: Multiplayer spreadsheet UI with active cell edits
Code snippet showing conflict resolution logic
Review mode toggle and proposal approval flow
missingEvidence: Direct console or network logs showing the WebSocket sync events during conflict resolution.

defects.observed: The code snippet is displayed with small text and is difficult to read within the short duration.
defects.fix: Increase the font size of the code block or extend the slide duration.

judge.suggestedReadmeCaption: Noderoom Live Collab demonstrates real-time multiplayer coordination between human users and AI agents editing a shared spreadsheet substrate with built-in conflict resolution.
